title: T-001 — Drizzle migration: wo_executions table + RLS
prompt:
# T-001 — Drizzle migration: wo_executions table + RLS

PRD: 08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §9.2, §5.5, §9.1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add Drizzle migration that creates `wo_executions` runtime state table per §9.2 with tenant RLS enforcement (5.1#6) and 7-year retention metadata (5.5).

## Implementation contract
Write Drizzle schema in packages/db/schema/production/wo-executions.ts mirroring the SQL in §9.2 (PK wo_id REFERENCES work_orders(id), tenant_id NOT NULL, started_at/completed_at, override_closed_production fields, gate_eval_snapshot JSONB). Create RLS policy `tenant_isolation_wo_executions` enabling SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE only when tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid. Add the two indexes from §9.2 (idx_wo_executions_tenant, idx_wo_executions_current_op partial WHERE completed_at IS NULL). Generate migration via `pnpm --filter db drizzle:generate` and verify it produces a single forward SQL.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/schema/production/wo-executions.ts [create]
- packages/db/schema/production/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_wo_executions.sql [create — generated]
- packages/db/__tests__/wo-executions-schema.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration applied, when `\d wo_executions` runs in psql, then all columns from §9.2 exist with NOT NULL where specified and JSONB for gate_eval_snapshot
2. Given a non-tenant connection, when SELECT * FROM wo_executions runs without app.tenant_id set, then RLS rejects the query
3. Given migration applied, when both indexes are queried via pg_indexes, then idx_wo_executions_tenant and idx_wo_executions_current_op exist with the partial predicate

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/wo-executions-schema.test.ts asserting Drizzle table has wo_id PK + tenant_id + RLS policy name before schema file exists
- Run `pnpm --filter db drizzle:generate && pnpm --filter db test` and capture migration SQL + test output

## Risk red lines
- Do not add columns beyond §9.2 (no scope creep)
- Do not skip RLS policy (multi-tenant data leak risk per R15)
- Do not edit other production tables in this task

pipeline_inputs.description: Drizzle migration creating wo_executions runtime state table with tenant RLS and §9.2 indexes.
pipeline_inputs.details: Mirror §9.2 SQL: PK wo_id REFERENCES work_orders(id), tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, timing fields (started_at/completed_at/cancelled_at), override fields (override_closed_production/override_reason/override_by), gate_eval_snapshot JSONB, current_operator_id UUID, total_pause_duration_min INTEGER DEFAULT 0. RLS policy via current_setting('app.tenant_id'). Two indexes: btree on tenant_id, partial on current_operator_id WHERE completed_at IS NULL. 7-year retention recorded as comment per §5.5.
out_of_scope: Do not create other production tables in this task
acceptance_criteria: Given migration applied, when '\d wo_executions' runs, then all §9.2 columns exist with correct nullability and gate_eval_snapshot is JSONB
Given non-tenant connection without app.tenant_id, when SELECT runs, then RLS blocks query with insufficient_privilege
Given migration applied, when pg_indexes queried, then idx_wo_executions_tenant and idx_wo_executions_current_op (with partial predicate) exist
test_strategy: RED: write packages/db/__tests__/wo-executions-schema.test.ts asserting Drizzle table shape + RLS policy name before schema file exists
Run 'pnpm --filter db drizzle:generate && pnpm --filter db test wo-executions-schema'
risk_red_lines: Do not skip RLS policy (R15 multi-tenant leak)
